title: Fetch-to-Commit Ingestion Unit, Checkpoints, and Partial Failure · Contract Triage
summary: A preserved Go feed-service run contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, normalization, fetch, SQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, handler, accessibility, migration, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in atomic feed ingestion unit of work and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Model claimed, fetching, fetched, parsed, normalized, committing, committed, failed, canceled, and superseded states.
Commit feed metadata, entries, revisions, validators, next schedule, and audit record together or preserve the prior admitted version.
Checkpoint only replay-safe boundaries and bind each checkpoint to source, validator, parser version, schema, and attempt identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Feed Go Ingestion State Machine
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 5230 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile fetch, parse, normalize, atomic commit, validators, checkpoints, and post-commit effects; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Model claimed, fetching, fetched, parsed, normalized, committing, committed, failed, canceled, and superseded states.

Inspect the Feed Go Ingestion State Machine trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Ingestion Reconciliation
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: feed-go-ingestion-reconciliation-1egves" that uses case 6798 to reconcile fetch, parse, normalize, atomic commit, validators, checkpoints, and post-commit effects. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, launch unbounded goroutines, or claim race, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, pgx 5.10.0, goose 3.27.2, PostgreSQL 18.4, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, disposable databases, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected images, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.
